tables.table_title: Accuracy (%) on multi-hop QA under language manipulation (clean retrieval)
tables.table_note: RAG drives the main gain (consistent with Yang et al., 2026). Explicit reasoning-language control further improves cross-lingual conditions, suggesting reasoning language is a meaningful factor (aligned with Li et al., 2026â€™s broader claim).

tables.table_title: Accuracy (%) under noisy retrieval (irrelevant + contradictory passages injected)
tables.table_note: Noise hurts all RAG variants; language control modestly helps robustness, but calibration-focused training is not primarily an accuracy booster.

tables.table_title: Calibration (ECE â†“) under clean vs noisy retrieval
tables.table_note: Consistent with Liu et al. (2026), retrieval noise inflates miscalibration. NAACL-style noise-aware calibration substantially reduces ECE and halves the noise-induced calibration degradation.

tables.table_title: Overconfidence on incorrect answers under contradictory retrieval (lower is better)
tables.table_note: The key safety improvement of ReaL-RAG is reducing false certainty in contradiction-heavy contexts (Liu et al., 2026).
